verses.commentary: Abraham moves south into the Negev. Gerar is Philistine territory. A new region, a repeated test.

Abraham repeats the deception from Egypt (12:13). She is my sister, a half-truth (verse 12). Abimelek takes Sarah. The promise is endangered again. Why does Abraham repeat this sin? Fear, habit, or testing of different territories.
God intervenes immediately. In a dream suggests divine communication to pagans. You are as good as dead. Because of the woman, literally she is married. God protects the promise.
Abimelek's defense. He had not gone near her (touched her sexually). Will you destroy an innocent nation? echoes Abraham's plea for Sodom (18:23). Abimelek argues for justice.
Abimelek's innocence. Both Abraham and Sarah lied. Clear conscience (literally integrity of my heart). Clean hands (innocence of hands). He acted in good faith based on false information.
God acknowledges Abimelek's innocence. I know. I have kept you from sinning. God's intervention prevented adultery. That is why I did not let you. Divine protection of both Abimelek and the promise.
Command to return Sarah. He is a prophet, first use of this word in Scripture. Abraham intercedes. If you do not return her, death is certain for you and all yours. The threat is total.
Immediate obedience. Early next morning. He tells his officials. They were very much afraid. Fear of God spreads through the pagan court.

Abimelek confronts Abraham. What have you done? The same question God asked Adam (3:13). How have I wronged you? Abimelek is the injured party. Great guilt, serious sin. Things that should never be done. The pagan king rebukes God's prophet.
What was your reason (literally what did you see)? Why did you lie?
Abraham's explanation. No fear of God. He assumed pagans had no morality. They will kill me. The same fear as in Egypt. Abraham misjudged and acted in fear, not faith.
Technical truth. Half-sister, same father, different mother. Such marriages were later forbidden (Leviticus 18:9, 20:17). And she became my wife. The full truth should have been told.

Abraham's long-standing scheme. When God had me wander. This has been their strategy everywhere. Show your love (chesed). He asked this of Sarah as a kindness. It's a systemic deception, not a one-time lapse.
Abimelek's generosity. Sheep, cattle, slaves. Lavish gifts despite being the injured party. He returned Sarah. Grace from the pagan king.
Freedom to stay. My land is before you. Live wherever you like. Remarkable hospitality after being deceived.

Brother with irony, exposing the lie. A thousand shekels is a huge sum. To cover the offense (literally to cover the eyes). You are completely vindicated (literally and with all you are righted). Abimelek publicly clears Sarah's honor.
Abraham intercedes as promised (verse 7). God healed. The healing includes fertility. Ironic that Abraham, who has no child yet, prays for others' fertility.

The reason for healing. The LORD had kept (literally closed every womb). A plague of barrenness fell on Abimelek's house. Because of Abraham's wife Sarah. This foreshadows Isaac's birth. God closes and opens wombs according to his purpose.
